step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("evidence on axitinib use in pancreatic cancer survival")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

After reviewing the retrieved evidence, I note that while axitinib shows activity in certain cancers like renal clear cell carcinoma and is part of combination regimens (with pembrolizumab) for advanced disease, there is **no direct evidence** linking axitinib to improved survival in pancreatic cancer patients. The only mention of axitinib relates to its use in renal cancer and combination therapy with immune checkpoint inhibitors—not in pancreatic cancer. Furthermore, targeted therapies such as sunitinib and everolimus have been evaluated in pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors, not diffuse ductal adenocarcinoma—which constitutes the majority of pancreatic cancer cases. Since axitinib lacks data demonstrating efficacy in pancreatic adenocarcinoma, particularly regarding survival improvement, my original conclusion stands.
\boxed{B}
